$comment: Reference case for aerodynamic_drag (F = 0.5 * Cd * airDensity * surfaceArea * vDotN). Three-input product-sweep with a single drag-coefficient to recover. Canonical Cd=0.47 is a flat plate / sphere; the library default matches so LM starts on the answer and must hold there across a 3D sweep.
notes: 36-point sweep (12 velocities × 3 areas × 1 density). F scales linearly with Cd so the fit is well-conditioned; relative_tolerance 1 % keeps the assertion tight while rmse_max=0.5 absorbs the O(10)-magnitude forces at the high-velocity end of the sweep.
